subject: 📊 Daily Portfolio: Markets closed | NVDA/AVGO near stops · UNCY PDUFA June 27
html_body:
Daily Portfolio Check · Weekend Edition

Sunday, June 14, 2026 · Kuwait time · NYSE closed

Portfolio Value (Fri Jun 12 close)

$823.63 (positions + cash)

Positions P&L

−$5.13 (−0.83%)

🎯 Withdrawal goal: $823.63 / $1,000 — $176.37 to go (82.4% there)

🚨 Weekend Alert — Two Positions Within 1% of Stop

NVDA: Closed Friday at $205.42 — just $1.42 above the $204 stop (0.7%). Low-volume tests near support are normal. A market-wide gap-down Monday could trigger it. Let the stop work — no pre-emptive action.
AVGO: Closed Friday at ~$382 — approximately $3 above the $379 stop (0.8%). Same risk profile at open. Both stops were set at real support levels — trust the plan.
If both stop out: combined loss approx −$28.96 on those two positions. TSM (+$7.33) and LLY (+$13.60) partially offset.

⌛ Pending — Waiting on IBKR Approval

VRDN Viridian Therapeutics WATCH 6/10

🔒 IBKR application pending

⚠️ PRICE DROPPED — Verify Thesis Still Intact

Current: $17.62  ·  Entry target: $20.00  ·  −11.9% below target
52-wk: $13.18–$34.29  ·  Stop: $14.50  ·  TP1: $28  ·  TP2: $37

💊 Veligrotug (IV IGF-1R antibody) for Thyroid Eye Disease — PDUFA June 30  🏽 SOON: 16 days

Approval probability: 82%

Halal: ⚠️ UNVERIFIED — check Musaffa (interest income ratio unclear)

Price has dropped 11.9% below the original $20 entry target. If IBKR approves before June 30, re-anchor entry to current price. At $17.62 entry with $14.50 stop: risk is 17.7% vs original 27.5% — better R:R if thesis intact. Conviction limits unchanged (no specialist fund Form 4 buys yet).

🚨 16 days to PDUFA. If IBKR not approved by ~June 23, this entry window closes. Run /stock-analyzer VRDN to re-derive levels at current price.

VERA Vera Therapeutics BUY NOW 7/10

🔒 IBKR application pending

Entry Window — Price Below Target (8.1%)

Current: $31.27  ·  Target: $34.04  ·  −8.1% (entry accessible)
New risk: Wedbush cut PT $34 → $26 (June 2026) — monitor for more downgrades.
Stop: $24.50  ·  TP1: $55  ·  TP2: $78

💊 Atacicept (dual BAFF/APRIL inhibitor) for IgA Nephropathy — PDUFA July 7  🏾 23 days

Approval probability: 89%

Halal: ⚠️ UNVERIFIED — check Musaffa (pre-revenue, term debt structure)

BTD + Priority Review + p<0.0001 Phase 3 + $5.28M director open-market buy. Zero CRLs in IgAN indication ever. At $31.27 entry (below original $34 target) R:R actually improves: 7-point stop vs 70% upside to TP1. Wedbush PT cut to $26 is the main new risk — check if it triggers consensus downshift.

When IBKR approved: enter $200–240 at market (~$31), stop $24.50. Entry at current price = better than original plan.

📡 Catalyst Plays — June 14, 2026

1 catalyst find (UNCY) · 2 large-cap fallback notes below

UNCY Unicycive Therapeutics🔮 INVESTIGATE 4/5

Catalyst: NDA resubmission PDUFA June 27, 2026 — 13 days away
Drug: Oxylanthanum Carbonate (OLC) — phosphate binder for hyperphosphatemia in CKD dialysis patients
Market cap: ~$146M  ·  Float: ~15.2M shares  ·  Short interest: ~8%
Price: ~$7.60–8.51 (last available, Jun 1–14)  ·  52-wk: $0.47–$8.74

Approval probability: ~65%

Manufacturing-only CRL (single third-party vendor deficiency — no clinical/safety concerns raised). Manufacturing-only resubmission base rate ~65–70%. CEO publicly optimistic pre-PDUFA.

✓ Approval: ~$15–20 (+100–150%) — analyst consensus $40.99; first-day target based on prior-resistance / CKD market TAM pricing
💀 Rejection: ~$3.00 (−60%) — typical CRL resubmission rejection drop; recovery floor near recent lows ($0.47 was 2025 bottom)
EV on $200 position at 65% prob: 0.65 × +$200 − 0.35 × $120 = +$88 expected / −$120 worst case

Smart money: Insiders bought $8.1M in last 3 months vs $146M market cap = 5.5% of market cap in accumulation. Strong signal ahead of PDUFA.
Cash runway: $42M (Q4 2025) — funded into 2027. Low dilution risk near-term.
Halal: ⚠️ UNVERIFIED — kidney disease drug, no obvious haram business activity. Check Musaffa for debt ratios before entering.

Say ‘analyze UNCY’ for the full 8-section deep dive before entering.

CELC Celcuity Inc. — large-cap fallback👁 WATCH

Market cap $5.45B — fails <$2B small-cap gate. Not sized as binary play. Showing as notable catalyst event.

Catalyst: Gedatolisib NDA PDUFA July 17, 2026 (33 days) · Priority Review
Indication: HR+/HER2−/PIK3CA wild-type advanced breast cancer
Data: VIKTORIA-1 mPFS 9.3 vs 5.0 months; hit primary endpoint. Strong efficacy and safety profile.
Cash: $441.5M+ funded through 2027. 9 analysts Strong Buy. Peak revenue guidance $2.5–3B.

Not a binary sizing candidate, but if you want oncology diversification the July PDUFA is the catalyst. Say ‘analyze CELC’ for full deep dive.

ASML ASML Holding — Tier A structural fallback👁 WATCH

No imminent binary catalyst. Pre-vetted Tier A halal pool. Structural rotation idea only.

EUV lithography monopoly — every process node transition at TSMC requires ASML equipment. You already own TSM; ASML is one layer deeper in the same AI capex supply chain. Adds semiconductor diversification without changing the AI thesis. Next earnings would be the nearest catalyst. Accumulate on dips rather than chasing.

💵 Cash Deployment — $213.81 available

After $3 reserve + $3 buy commission: $207.81 net deployable

⚠ Context: NVDA and AVGO are both within 1% of stops. Deploying cash before Monday open adds binary risk on top of potential stop-out risk. Factor this in.

Option A — UNCY catalyst play (if Musaffa clears):

Buy 26 shares at ~$7.80 = $202.80 + $3 commission = $205.80 total. Cash remaining: $8.01 (above $3 floor). Position = 24.6% of $823 portfolio (within 30% cap). PDUFA June 27. EV at 65%: +$88. Risk: −$120.
Requires Musaffa halal verification first.

Option B — Hold as dry powder (recommended for now):

Keep $213.81 in cash until Monday open confirms NVDA/AVGO stops are not threatened. If both hold, UNCY entry is valid Monday with 12 days to PDUFA. If one stops out, you have ~$280–420 to redeploy into a better-priced position (potentially VERA or VRDN at better levels).

Recommended: Option B — hold through open. Re-assess Monday 9:35 AM ET. Your call.

What to do Monday morning

1. Watch NVDA ($204 stop) and AVGO ($379 stop) at the open — no need to babysit, stops execute automatically.
2. Check Musaffa for UNCY halal status before placing any order.
3. If market opens +0.3% or better and both stops hold: consider UNCY entry at market, 12 days to PDUFA.
4. Check VERA for additional analyst commentary after Wedbush PT cut ($34 → $26) — if more downgrades follow, reassess conviction.

📚 Today you learned — Concept #18

Short Interest & Squeeze Setups

Short interest is the percentage of shares currently sold short. Above 20% is elevated; above 40% is squeeze territory. Came up today: UNCY sits at ~8% short interest — meaningful but not squeeze-level. The mechanic: shorts borrow shares and sell them, expecting to buy back cheaper. When the stock rises instead, brokers force covering (buying back), which drives price higher, forcing more covering — self-feeding. Unlike FX where you can short limitlessly, equity shorts must borrow real shares from holders. UNCY’s 15.2M float means fewer available shares — any approval surprise amplifies the move. Your core holdings are near-zero short interest (NVDA ~1%, AVGO ~2%), so this plays out in opportunistic names like catalyst plays, not your main book.

Also today: Float — shares actually available for trading (total shares minus locked insider/insider-lockup holdings). UNCY’s 15.2M float is what makes a squeeze structurally possible if the PDUFA is a surprise to the 8% short base.

Research output, not financial advice. Past performance does not guarantee future results. Verify halal compliance on Musaffa before entering any new position. Prices: Yahoo Finance, Robinhood, Investing.com, Stockanalysis.com — Fri Jun 12, 2026 close. AVGO approximate ($377–388 range). Generated Sun Jun 14, 2026, Kuwait time.
